unit.comment: /* * Created by Phil on 29/11/2010. * Copyright 2010 Two Blue Cubes Ltd. All rights reserved. * * Distributed under the Boost Software License, Version 1.0. (See accompanying * file LICENSE_1_0.txt or copy at http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt) */

function.name: testCheckedIf
testCheckedElse

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("A couple of nested sections followed by a failure", "[failing][.]")

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "toString on const wchar_t const pointer returns the string contents", "[toString]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "toString on const wchar_t pointer returns the string contents", "[toString]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "toString on wchar_t const pointer returns the string contents", "[toString]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "toString on wchar_t returns the string contents", "[toString]" )

diff:delete.comment: // int x = 10/i; // This should cause the signal to fire

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "# A test name that starts with a #" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#835 -- errno should not be touched by Catch", "[.][failing][!shouldfail]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#961 -- Dynamically created sections should all be reported", "[.]" )
